samples.gvd:
module div_8bit (
    input wire clk,
    input wire rst,
    input wire [7:0] dividend,
    input wire [7:0] divisor,
    input wire sign,
    input wire opn_valid,
    output reg res_valid,
    input wire res_ready,
    output reg [15:0] result
);
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</implementation>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Idle state where the module waits for a valid operation request.</state>
        <state id="DIVIDE">State where the division operation is performed.</state>
        <state id="DONE">State where the division operation is completed.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="dividend_reg" width="8" description="Register to hold the dividend." />
        <reg id="divisor_reg" width="8" description="Register to hold the divisor." />
        <reg id="result_reg" width="16" description="Register to hold the result." />
        <reg id="start_cnt" width="1" description="Flag to indicate the start of the division process." />
        <reg id="cnt" width="4" description="Counter to track the number of iterations." />
        <reg id="NEG_DIVISOR" width="9" description="Negative divisor for subtraction." />
        <reg id="SR" width="9" description="Shift register for storing partial results." />
        <wire id="carry_out" width="1" description="Carry out signal from subtraction." />
        <wire id="sub_result" width="9" description="Result of subtraction." />
        <mux id="mux_result" width="9" description="Multiplexer to select between subtraction result and SR." />
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                dividend_reg <= 8'b0;
                divisor_reg <= 8'b0;
                result_reg <= 16'b0;
                start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                cnt <= 4'b0;
                NEG_DIVISOR <= 9'b0;
                SR <= 9'b0;
                carry_out <= 1'b0;
                sub_result <= 9'b0;
                mux_result <= 9'b0;
                res_valid <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin
                    dividend_reg <= dividend;
                    divisor_reg <= divisor;
                    start_cnt <= 1'b1;
                    cnt <= 4'b1;
                    NEG_DIVISOR <= {1'b1, ~divisor + 1};
                    SR <= {1'b0, dividend << 1};
                end else if (start_cnt) begin
                    if (cnt[3]) begin
                        start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                        result_reg <= {SR[8], SR[7:0]};
                    end else begin
                        cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        sub_result <= SR - NEG_DIVISOR;
                        carry_out <= sub_result[8];
                        mux_result <= carry_out ? sub_result : SR;
                        SR <= {mux_result[8], mux_result[7:0], 1'b0};
                    end
                end
                if (rst) begin
                    res_valid <= 1'b0;
                end else if (cnt == 4'b1000) begin
                    res_valid <= 1'b1;
                end else if (res_valid && res_ready) begin
                    res_valid <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
</module>
